rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem trifluoromethylation–fluorination of styrenes using CF3SO2Na as CF3 source and Et3N·3HF as fluoride source to give β-fluoro-α-trifluoromethyl products
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF3SO2Na at carbon rod anode generates CF3 radical (with loss of SO2); the benzylic radical adduct is further anodically oxidized to a benzylic carbocation; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical generated from anodic oxidation/desulfonylation of CF3SO2Na
Benzylic carbon radical I from CF3 addition to styrene C=C
Benzylic carbocation II from anodic oxidation of radical I

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF3SO2Na with loss of SO2 to give CF3 radical, which adds to the styrene C=C to form benzylic radical I; I is further oxidized at the anode to benzylic cation II, which is trapped by fluoride from Et3N·3HF; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely shut down, but no TEMPO–CF3 adduct detected by GC-MS; attributed to TEMPO being oxidized to TEMPO+ at lower potential than CF3SO2Na

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction shut down; BHT–CF3 adduct 3a detected by GC-MS, supporting CF3 radical

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Both addition product 3b and coupling product 3c detected by GC-MS, corroborating radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: CF3SO2Na (CV probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation peak at 1.14 V vs Ag/AgNO3 confirms CF3SO2Na is anodically oxidized first; pink trace with 1a shows catalytic current

probe_reagents.name: TBACl (CV control)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation of Cl- at 1.44 V matches 1.37 V peak, assigned to chloride from DCE substitution

notes: Authors propose radical–polar crossover: radical addition followed by anodic oxidation to cation, then F- nucleophilic addition. TEMPO failed as trap due to its lower oxidation potential than CF3SO2Na (ineffective TEMPO/TEMPO+ conversion); BHT and DPE successfully trapped the CF3 radical. CV also revealed a side oxidation peak at 1.37 V assigned to chloride generated by nucleophilic substitution of DCE solvent by CF3SO2Na.
